turns.text: Thanks for calling Sears Home Services — what appliance can I help you with today?
Hi, my Samsung washer is flashing a code and I don't know what it means.
No problem — on your washer that will be a Whirlpool-style fault code, an F code paired with an E code, like F5 E2.
It's definitely a Samsung — the code has a letter and a number.
Right, all these washers use the same Whirlpool F and E code system since they share the Whirlpool parts family, so yours works exactly the same way.
Okay, so what should I do about it?

Since your machine is Whirlpool-built, unplug it for one minute to reset the Whirlpool control board — that clears F and E codes on these units. Whirlpool boards are very forgiving, so it should come right back.
